text:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Elaine Chou <elaine.chou@brightmind.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-05-22T08:14:00-07:00
Subject: Quill3b elevated 5xxs during morning window — quick update

Elaine —

We observed an elevated rate of 5xx and timeout errors for the Quill3b model serving your production traffic between 07:50–08:30 PDT this morning. Error rate peaked at ~12% for token-generation requests and correlated with increased p99 latency. We've attached a Grafana snapshot and a short request trace for your team to review.

Immediate actions we've taken:
- Traffic was temporarily routed to Quill-mini fallback model at 08:12 PDT to reduce customer impact.
- Ops team triggered a rolling restart of the Quill3b runtime pool and disabled a recent kernel patch suspected of increasing KV cache churn.

Requested...

We're seeing a rapid increase in p99 latency (from ~180ms to >1.6s) and a spike in 500/5...
text:
From: Samantha Reed <samantha@quantumbridge.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Amit Patel <amit.patel@quantumbridge.io>
Date: Mon, 14 May 2028 13:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production degradation - latency & 5xx surge

Ben —

We're seeing a rapid increase in p99 latency (from ~180ms to >1.6s) and a spike in 500/502 responses starting ~11:50 PDT this morning. Throughput for our main routing key increased ~3x which coincided with numerous token-level retries from our side. Our SREs captured a tail of many KV eviction events and significant queueing on the inference nodes. This is impacting three critical customer flows and we've had session failures in the storefront checkout path.

Can Redwood provide immediate mitigation and a timeline for a fix? We need an ETA for restoring p99 under 400ms and eliminating the 5xx burst.

We've attached a short snapshot (throughput-snapshot-2028051...

Cc: Priya Shah <priya@luminedge.com>
Date: Tue, 07 May 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Production incident — May 6-7 token and latency spike

Hi Aditya,

We saw repeated 5-10s tail latencies and token throttles across our nightly inference jobs between 2028-05-06 22:30 UTC and 2028-05-07 03:15 UTC. This manifested as elevated error rates and several timeouts for customer-facing flows. We logged it as INC-2497 on our side.

Can you share what happened on Redwood's side, whether you have remediation logs, and whether there's scope for a credit/compensation for the impacted tokens? The outage caused a visible regression for a subset of our customers and we had to route traffic to a fallback.

Thanks — we need a ballpark by EOD to brief our leadership.

Jordan Park
Site Reliability Manager, LuminEdge

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the walkthrough this morning. Short version: our production model traffic is dominated by user inference during the day, but we also run a large nightly batch that backs the recommendation index (approx. 50M tokens in a 90-minute window). Over the last two months we've seen that this batch occasionally pushes our reserved GPUs well above the concurrency profile assumed in the contract, which triggers preemption and tail latency hits for live traffic.

What we are asking for: a time-boxed carveout (nightly 02:00–03:30 UTC, Mon/Wed/Fri) where the dedicated pool will allow short bursts above the usual per-tenant throughput floor without triggering aggressive preemption, in exchange for a firm ...

- Success criteria we should validate during the trial: end-to-end latency under 300ms p90 for in-app calls, model precision/recall targets tied to the provided prompt set, and integration stability for 1000 daily users.

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the quick intro on the call earlier. We need Redwood set up as a registered supplier in Trinova's AP system before we can process invoices against customer POs. Attached is our vendor onboarding packet with the required fields. High-level checklist: 

- Complete vendor profile in our portal (legal name, remit address, VAT/EORI or Tax ID, bank routing and account).
- Upload signed tax docs (W-9 for US entity, EORI/VAT certificate for EU entity).
- Provide a PDF sample invoice and a 1-line test invoice showing PO placement.
- Important: Trinova requires the customer PO number to appear in the invoice header and in the e-invoice metadata...
